calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Walker for John Flynn.

Mr. Walker stated that a response to the Probate Examiner's notes was filed on January 3rd (copy provided), that he is unable to provide timesheets from the Personal Representative as requested by the Examiner because his Client did not keep detailed records and that all of the Personal Representative's time was based on his travel time. Mr. Walker also stated that there was a hoarding issue, that his Client was putting in a full day every time he came to San Francisco, that his Client chose not to stay in a hotel because of the high cost, that he made 104 trips and that his Client spent a number of hours cleaning out the house. The Court noted that the statutory fees are at $27,900.00, that the extraordinary fees are at $39,000.00 and explained to Counsel that in order for the Court to approve extraordinary fees it needs to look at the work that was done and the time spent doing the work to make sure there are no overpayments. The Court also stated that it will have to reduce the extraordinary fees, that reimbursement for mileage is not allowed and that it's the same issue with the Attorney's fees. Mr. Walker stated that he is now at 86 hours for ordinary fees. The Amended First and Final Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
